summary: Recent updates in the session negotiation area of the Firefox browser have shifted the codebase to make better use of smart pointers, specifically **UniquePtrs**. This change enhances memory management, ensuring that memory is automatically cleaned up when it's no longer needed, which helps prevent memory leaks and improves performance. Additionally, the updates have standardized the way certain attributes are handled in the SDP (Session Description Protocol), which is crucial for managing multimedia sessions like video calls. By transitioning to more modern C++ practices and improving code consistency, these changes contribute to a more reliable and maintainable WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) implementation, ultimately enhancing users' calling experience.
